channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Success Lab: Motivation & Change II
item.description: Date: Tuesday, March 24, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Do you put things off until the last minute only to be disappointed by the outcome? Learn how to develop and maintain motivation to achieve goals to make change in your life. Whether you want to spend more time on school work or learn to play the piano, making change requires a specific set of steps to ensure success.
item.author: Students

item.title: Passover Kosher Meals - Sign Up before March 26th
item.description: Passover is approaching, and we are now accepting sign-ups for Kosher meals for Passover. Please make sure to register no later than Thursday, March 26, 2026. Click the link above for more information and how to sign up.
item.author: Students

item.title: March Events with Rensselaer Dining
item.description: There’s a lot happening this March—join us for great food, fun events, and opportunities to connect with the campus dining community. Click the link above for the full schedule of events!
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Hosting Review Sessions Week of 3/22-3/29
item.description: Need some last minute review for your upcoming exam? Stop by for a Puck Cookie and a Review Session with our Success Center Tutors! Physics I: Sunday, 3/22 (3-5pm) at the Success Center Thermodynamics: Sunday, 3/22 (5-7pm) at the Success Center MATH 1520 Math Methods: Tuesday, 3/24 (5-7pm) Blitman Classroom in ECAV (2nd floor) Diff Eq.: Tuesday, 3/24 (5-7pm) at the Success Center
item.author: Students

item.title: More courses being add to Tutoring in Nason
item.description: Did you know we offer tutoring from our Success Center Tutors in Nason Hall? Check it out: -Physics I & II- Mondays, 7-9pm -CAD - Mondays, 7-9pm -CAL I & II -Tuesdays, 7-9pm -CS I and Data Structures - Wednesdays, 7-9pm
item.author: Students

item.title: MARCH - APRIL Mercer X CLUBHOUSE WORKSHOP Events
item.description: Bring the heat with March - April Mercer X Workshops: Learn skills to Maximize Your Summer (Andrew Morrison ’87). Design an Integrated Circuit (IEEE) or a Pixie Radio with an Antenna (W2SZ). Learn from experts about cutting-edge AI technology (RPAi). Create your own custom keypad to learn PCB design and physically make it using the Mercer X Printed Circuit Board Prototyping Suite (IEEE). RSVP for these free events held by Mercer X Affiliated Clubs!
item.author: Students

item.title: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals 2027 Co-op Program Info Session
item.description: Regeneron is a leading biotechnology company that invents, develops, and commercializes life-transforming medicines for people with serious diseases.Virtual Info Session:Wednesday, March 25th - 7:00pm ESTExplore Bold ideas. Go deep into your passion. And learn from people at the top of their field. As a Regeneron Co-op, you’ll get hands-on, real-world experience in biotech.Our Co-oppositions are full-time from January to August (Spring-Summer), May/June-December (Summer-Fall), and August…
item.author: Students

item.title: Webinar Friday March 27: RPI Digital Asset Management (DAM) System
item.description: Join Jim Evans, Director of MultiMedia Services, on Friday March 27 from 11am to 12pm, for a live webinar introducing Rensselaer’s Digital Asset Management System. Discover how to access campus‑wide photography, multimedia files, and institutional assets—all in one place. Register now: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Team Places 4th in Lockheed Martin Ethics in Engineering Case Competition!
item.description: In February two RPI undergraduate students, Nyati Misra ’27 (BMED) and John Weller ’27 (AERO), placed in the Top 4 out of 72 schools nationwide! They represented RPI with exceptional analytical rigor, professionalism, and ethical depth. Throughout the competition, they demonstrated remarkable composure, clarity of thought, and principled decision-making, serving as outstanding ambassadors for RPI. Dr. Azita Hirsa served as their faculty advisor for this competition. To learn more about the…
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Ranks Among Nation’s Top Universities for Patents
item.description: Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute (RPI) has been ranked 50th on the National Academy of Inventors’ (NAI) 2025 Top 100 U.S. Universities Granted U.S. Utility Patents list, reaffirming the Institute’s commitment to innovation and the translation of cutting-edge research into real-world impact.
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Workshop: RPI MAX for Staff: Leveraging Feedback and Career Opportunities
item.description: Date: Thursday, March 26, 2026 8:00 AM - 9:30 AM - As an employee, you work in partnership with your manager to align expectations and set meaningful goals. Throughout the year, you check in together, recalibrate as needed, and celebrate progress and achievements. This workshop focuses on supporting your individual career development, preparing for the annual manager evaluation, setting effective goals, and using tools that can help you advance your career.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Workshop: RPI MAX for Supervisors: Performance and Coaching
item.description: Date: Thursday, March 26, 2026 10:00 AM - 12:00 PM - As a manager, you play a central role in guiding the performance process for your employees—working collaboratively to align expectations, support development, and monitor progress toward achieving goals. This workshop introduces the core principles of performance coaching, including how to identify and evaluate performance gaps, set effective goals, and lead the RPI MAX performance process. Participants will also explore practical tools for…
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Workshop: “Getting Started with Percipio: Navigation & Q&A”
item.description:
This brief course is designed to help you get comfortable using Percipio, RPI’s digital learning platform. You’ll learn how to navigate the main dashboard, search for content, access assigned learning, and track your progress. We’ll also walk through common questions users have and show you where to find help when needed. Whether you’re brand new or just need a refresher, this session will ensure you’re ready to make the most of Percipio.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
